title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement gmboggio@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Introduction to greenfeedr
text: first step working GreenFeed system retrieve data. can done manually logging GreenFeed web interface C-Lock Inc. However, easiest way retrieve daily data via API using get_gfdata() function. function automates data retrieval process, allowing specify user password, units, date range download data directly computer.

title: Introduction to greenfeedr
text: explore basic data manipulation functions greenfeedr, ’ll use dairy cow data experiment 32 cows.
code: data <- readr::read_csv(system.file("extdata", "StudyName_GFdata.csv", package = "greenfeedr"), show_col_types = FALSE) dim(data) #> [1] 256  21 data #> # A tibble: 256 × 21 #>    FeederID AnimalName   RFID  StartTime EndTime GoodDataDuration CO2GramsPerDay #>       <dbl> <chr>        <chr> <chr>     <chr>   <time>                    <dbl> #>  1        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 02'31"                   10531. #>  2        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 06'09"                   14032. #>  3        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 10'21"                    9242. #>  4        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 04'00"                   14735. #>  5        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 04'55"                   20080. #>  6        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 03'42"                   13905. #>  7        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 02'12"                   18625. #>  8        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 02'23"                   15462. #>  9        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 05'05"                   15689. #> 10        1 "\"00000000… 0000… 5/13/24 … 5/13/2… 02'16"                   18011. #> # ℹ 246 more rows #> # ℹ 14 more variables: CH4GramsPerDay <dbl>, O2GramsPerDay <dbl>, #> #   H2GramsPerDay <dbl>, H2SGramsPerDay <dbl>, AirflowLitersPerSec <dbl>, #> #   AirflowCf <dbl>, WindSpeedMetersPerSec <dbl>, WindDirDeg <dbl>, #> #   WindCf <dbl>, WasInterrupted <lgl>, InterruptingTags <chr>, #> #   TempPipeDegreesCelsius <dbl>, IsPreliminary <dbl>, RunTime <chr>

title: Authors and Citation
text: Guillermo Martinez-Boggio. Maintainer, author, copyright holder. Meredith Harrison. Contributor. Patrick Lutz. Contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Martinez-Boggio G (2024). greenfeedr: Process Report GreenFeed Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://gmbog.github.io/greenfeedr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {greenfeedr: Process and Report GreenFeed Data},   author = {Guillermo Martinez-Boggio},   year = {2024},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://gmbog.github.io/greenfeedr/}, }

title: Process and Report GreenFeed Data
text: greenfeedr provides set functions help work GreenFeed data: get_gfdata() downloads GreenFeed data via API. report_gfdata() downloads generates markdown reports daily final GreenFeed data. process_gfdata() processes averages daily final GreenFeed data. pellin() processes pellet intakes GreenFeed units. viseat() processes GreenFeed visits. use daily final data GreenFeed system.

title: Process and Report GreenFeed Data
text: complete information use greenfeedr can found : …

title: Process and Report GreenFeed Data
text: can install development version greenfeedr GitHub :

title: Process and Report GreenFeed Data
text: present example use process_gfdata(): Note received finalized data (Summarized Data) study using GreenFeed C-Lock Inc. , now need process daily records obtained. data looks like: first step investigate total number records, records per day, days records per week GreenFeed data. use process_gfdata() function test 3 threshold values define records retain analysis. Note function includes 3 parameters: - param1: number records per day. - parameter controls minimum number records must present day dataset considered valid. - param2: number days records per week. - parameter ensures minimum number days within week valid records included analysis. - min_time: minimum duration record. - parameter specifies minimum time threshold record considered valid. can make iterative process evaluating possible combinations parameters. , define parameters follows: Interestingly, 60 combinations 3 parameters (param1, param2, min_time). next step, evaluate function process_gfdata() defined set parameters. Note function can handle argument file path data files data data frame. Finally, results function placed data frame following structure: Also, possible compute Pearson correlations different parameters number records, /gas production average. gives user idea pros cons less conservative processing GreenFeed data analysis. general, conservative parameters , fewer records retained data.
code: library(greenfeedr) #> # A tibble: 5 × 25 #>   RFID                 `Farm Name`   FID `Start Time`        `End Time`          #>   <chr>                <chr>       <dbl> <dttm>              <dttm>              #> 1 0000000008400032506… 0000000008…     1 2024-05-13 09:33:24 2024-05-13 09:36:31 #> 2 0000000008400032506… 0000000008…     1 2024-05-13 10:25:44 2024-05-13 10:32:40 #> 3 0000000008400032506… 0000000008…     1 2024-05-13 12:29:02 2024-05-13 12:45:19 #> 4 0000000008400032506… 0000000008…     1 2024-05-13 13:06:20 2024-05-13 13:12:14 #> 5 0000000008400032506… 0000000008…     1 2024-05-13 14:34:58 2024-05-13 14:41:52 #> # ℹ 20 more variables: `Good Data Duration` <dttm>, `Hour Of Day` <dbl>, #> #   `CO2 Massflow (g/d)` <dbl>, `CH4 Massflow (g/d)` <dbl>, #> #   `O2 Massflow (g/d)` <dbl>, `H2 Massflow (g/d)` <dbl>, #> #   `H2S Massflow (g/d)` <dbl>, `Average Airflow (L/s)` <dbl>, #> #   `Airflow CF` <dbl>, `Average Wind Speed (m/s)` <dbl>, #> #   `Average Wind Direction (deg)` <dbl>, `Wind CF` <dbl>, #> #   `Was Interrupted` <lgl>, `Interrupting Tags` <chr>, … # Define the parameter space for param1 (i), param2 (j), and min_time (k): i <- seq(1, 3) j <- seq(3, 7) k <- seq(2, 5)  # Generate all combinations of i, j, and k param_combinations <- expand.grid(param1 = i, param2 = j, min_time = k) # Helper function to call process_gfdata and extract relevant information process_and_summarize <- function(param1, param2, min_time) {   data <- process_gfdata(     data = finaldata,     start_date = "2024-05-13",     end_date = "2024-05-25",     param1 = param1,     param2 = param2,     min_time = min_time   )    # Extract daily_data and weekly_data   daily_data <- data$daily_data   weekly_data <- data$weekly_data    # Calculate the required metrics   records_d <- nrow(daily_data)   cows_d <- length(unique(daily_data$RFID))    mean_dCH4 <- mean(daily_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   sd_dCH4 <- sd(daily_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   CV_dCH4 <- sd(daily_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(daily_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   mean_dCO2 <- mean(daily_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   sd_dCO2 <- sd(daily_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   CV_dCO2 <- sd(daily_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(daily_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)    records_w <- nrow(weekly_data)   cows_w <- length(unique(weekly_data$RFID))    mean_wCH4 <- mean(weekly_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   sd_wCH4 <- sd(weekly_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   CV_wCH4 <- sd(weekly_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(weekly_data$CH4GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   mean_wCO2 <- mean(weekly_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   sd_wCO2 <- sd(weekly_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)   CV_wCO2 <- sd(weekly_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(weekly_data$CO2GramsPerDay, na.rm = TRUE)    # Return a summary row   return(data.frame(     param1 = param1,     param2 = param2,     min_time = min_time,     records_d = records_d,     cows_d = cows_d,     mean_dCH4 = round(mean_dCH4, 1),     sd_dCH4 = round(sd_dCH4, 1),     CV_dCH4 = round(CV_dCH4, 2),     mean_dCO2 = round(mean_dCO2, 1),     sd_dCO2 = round(sd_dCO2, 1),     CV_dCO2 = round(CV_dCO2, 2),     records_w = records_w,     cows_w = cows_w,     mean_wCH4 = round(mean_wCH4, 1),     sd_wCH4 = round(sd_wCH4, 1),     CV_wCH4 = round(CV_wCH4, 2),     mean_wCO2 = round(mean_wCO2, 1),     sd_wCO2 = round(sd_wCO2, 1),     CV_wCO2 = round(CV_wCO2, 2)   )) } head(data) #>   param1 param2 min_time records_d cows_d mean_dCH4 sd_dCH4 CV_dCH4 mean_dCO2 #> 1      1      3        2       184     25     380.3   110.5    0.29   11426.1 #> 2      2      3        2       116     20     380.9    85.0    0.22   11445.8 #> 3      3      3        2        75     19     373.3    87.0    0.23   11386.8 #> 4      1      4        2       184     25     380.3   110.5    0.29   11426.1 #> 5      2      4        2       116     20 what: Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
title: Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
text: Helper function check date format.     format wrong 'NA' error message printed, else     formatted date correct way (YYYY-MM-DD)

title: Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
text: date_input Date included input

title: Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
text: character string representing date 'YYYY-MM-DD' format

title: Check date format and transform in a usable one — ensure_date_format
code: # Example of correct date formats ensure_date_format("2024-08-30") # "2024-08-30" #> [1] "2024-08-30" ensure_date_format("08/30/2024") # "2024-08-30" #> [1] "2024-08-30" ensure_date_format("30/08/2024") # "2024-08-30" #> [1] "2024-08-30"  # Example of incorrect date formats tryCatch({   ensure_date_format("Aug-30") }, error = function(e) {   message(e$message) }) #> Error processing date: Invalid date format. Please provide a recognizable date format.

what: Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
title: Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
text: Helper function detect outliers using mean sd

title: Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
text: v vector data cutoff threshold cutoff value defines range (e.g., 2.5)

title: Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
text: logical vector length `v`, element `TRUE` corresponding value `v` falls within specified range, `FALSE` otherwise.

title: Detect outliers in data using mean and standard deviation — filter_within_range
code: # Sample data data <- c(10, 12, 14, 15, 20, 25, 30, 100)  # Detect values within 2.5 standard deviations from the mean filter_within_range(data, cutoff = 2.5) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE  # Result: # [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE # Explanation: All values fall within 2.5 standard deviations from the mean.  # Detect values within 1 standard deviation from the mean filter_within_range(data, cutoff = 1) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  # Result: # [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE FALSE # Explanation: All values except 100 fall within 1 standard deviation from the mean.

what: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
title: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
text: Downloads C-Lock Inc. server daily GreenFeed data     via application programming interface (API).     Retrieves data based specified parameters login information,     date range units, providing Excel file GreenFeed data.

title: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
code: get_gfdata(   user,   pass,   exp = NA,   unit,   start_date,   end_date = Sys.Date(),   save_dir = getwd() )

title: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
text: user User name log GreenFeed system pass Password log GreenFeed system exp Study name study identifier. used file name save data unit GreenFeed unit number(s). multiple units, vector, list, character "1,2" start_date Start date study end_date End date study. default current date used save_dir Directory save output file. default current working directory used

title: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
text: function return value. saves downloaded data CSV file specified directory.

title: Download Daily GreenFeed Data via API — get_gfdata
code: if (FALSE) { # has_credentials() # Please replace "your_username" and "your_password" with your actual GreenFeed credentials. user <- Sys.getenv("API_USER") pass <- Sys.getenv("API_PASS") exp <- "StudyName" start_date <- "2024-01-01" end_date <- Sys.Date() save_dir <- tempdir()  # Example with multiple units as a comma-separated string unit <- "304,305" get_gfdata(user, pass, exp, unit, start_date, end_date, save_dir)  # Example with a single unit as a numeric value unit <- 304 get_gfdata(user, pass, exp, unit, start_date, end_date, save_dir)  # Example with units as a vector unit <- c(304, 305) get_gfdata(user, pass, exp, unit, start_date, end_date, save_dir) }

title: Check for API Credentials — has_credentials
text: Helper function check necessary     API credentials available environment.

title: Check for API Credentials — has_credentials
text: logical value: `TRUE` `API_USER` `API_PASS` environment variables set (.e., `NA`); `FALSE` otherwise

title: Check for API Credentials — has_credentials
code: # Example 1: When environment variables are set Sys.setenv(API_USER = "my_username", API_PASS = "my_password") has_credentials() #> [1] TRUE # Expected output: TRUE  # Example 2: When one or both environment variables are not set Sys.unsetenv("API_USER") Sys.unsetenv("API_PASS") has_credentials() #> [1] FALSE # Expected output: FALSE  # Clean up by removing environment variables Sys.unsetenv("API_USER") Sys.unsetenv("API_PASS")

title: Process GreenFeed Pellet Intakes — pellin
text: Processes feedtimes file GreenFeed system,     including visits food drops across specific period, used     calculate pellet intakes per animal units.     Aggregates data provide insights feeding behavior     pellet consumption animals study.

title: Process GreenFeed Pellet Intakes — pellin
text: file_path File feedtimes C-Lock. multiple files provided, units order unit GreenFeed unit number(s). multiple units, vector, list, character "1,2" gcup Grams pellets per cup. default 34 grams, users provide grams based 10-drops test start_date Start date study end_date End date study save_dir Directory save resulting file pellet intakes rfid_file file contains rfid animals enrolled study. order col1=FarmName col2=RFID

title: Process GreenFeed Pellet Intakes — pellin
text: Excel file pellet intakes animals days within specified period saved `save_dir`.     file named "Pellet_Intakes_YYYY-MM-DD_YYYY-MM-DD.csv".

title: Process GreenFeed Pellet Intakes — pellin
code: # You should provide the 'feedtimes' file provided by C-Lock. # it could be a list of files if you have data from multiple units to combine file <- list(system.file("extdata", "feedtimes.csv", package = "greenfeedr"))  # By default the function use 34g, but you should include the result obtained from the 10-drops test  # If the user include an rfid file, the structure should be in col1 the farmname or visualID, and # col2 the RFID or TAG_ID. The file could be save in different formats (.xlsx, .csv, or .txt).  pellin(file,   unit = 1,   gcup = 34,   start_date = "2024-05-13",   end_date = "2024-05-25",   save_dir = tempdir() ) #> Please set the 'gcup' parameter based on the 10-drops test. The default value is 34g. #> RFID is NULL. It is recommended to include it. #> Pellet intakes file created and saved to /tmp/RtmpOT62S6

what: Process Daily and Final GreenFeed Data — process_gfdata
title: Process Daily and Final GreenFeed Data — process_gfdata
text: Processes computes daily weekly averages     daily final GreenFeed data, organizing two data sets.     Handles data filtering, aggregation, summarization facilitate     analysis reporting.

title: Process Daily and Final GreenFeed Data — process_gfdata
text: data Data frame daily finalized GreenFeed data start_date Start date study end_date End date study param1 Number records per day consider analysis param2 Number days records per week consider analysis min_time Minimum number minutes records consider analysis. default min_time 2

title: Process Daily and Final GreenFeed Data — process_gfdata
text: list two data frames: daily_data Data frame daily processed GreenFeed data weekly_data Data frame weekly processed GreenFeed data

title: Process Daily and Final GreenFeed Data — process_gfdata
Process RFID Data — process_rfid_data
text: Processes RFID data animals study

title: Process RFID Data — process_rfid_data
text: rfid_file Path data frame containing RFID data.

title: Process RFID Data — process_rfid_data
text: data frame standardized column names (`FarmName` `RFID`). input invalid valid data provided, function returns `NULL`.

title: Process RFID Data — process_rfid_data
code: # Example with a data frame df <- data.frame(   V1 = c("Farm1", "Farm2", "Farm3"),   V2 = c("12345", "67890", "54321") ) processed_df <- process_rfid_data(df) print(processed_df) #>   FarmName  RFID #> 1    Farm1 12345 #> 2    Farm2 67890 #> 3    Farm3 54321  # Example with invalid input invalid_data <- process_rfid_data(NULL) #> RFID is NULL. It is recommended to include it. # Expected output: message "RFID is NA. It is recommended to include it." and NULL print(invalid_data) #> NULL  # Example with unsupported file format # Assuming 'rfid_data.docx' is an unsupported file format invalid_file <- process_rfid_data("path/to/rfid_data.docx") #> No valid data provided. Please include a valid 'rfid_file' parameter. # Expected output: error message "Unsupported file format." print(invalid_file) #> NULL

what: Download and Report GreenFeed Data — report_gfdata
title: Download and Report GreenFeed Data — report_gfdata
text: Generates PDF reports daily final GreenFeed data.     option "daily" used, data retrieved C-Lock server via application programming interface (API)     generates PDF report number animals, records, gas production ongoing study.     , option "final" used, final data provided generates PDF report     evaluate GreenFeed data obtained finalized study.

title: Download and Report GreenFeed Data — report_gfdata
text: user User name log GreenFeed system pass Password log GreenFeed system exp Study name study identifier. used file name report data unit GreenFeed unit number(s). multiple units, vector, list, character "1,2" start_date Start date study end_date End date study. default current date used input_type Input data daily final report: daily final save_dir Directory save output file. default current working directory used plot_opt Type gas plot: , CH4, CO2, O2, H2. default CH4 processed reported rfid_file File contains RFID animals study file_path List files final report GreenFeed

title: Download and Report GreenFeed Data — report_gfdata
text: CSV file daily GreenFeed data PDF report description daily final records.

title: Download and Report GreenFeed Data — report_gfdata
code: if (FALSE) { # has_credentials() # Please replace "your_username" and "your_password" with your actual GreenFeed credentials. user <- Sys.getenv("API_USER") pass <- Sys.getenv("API_PASS")  # The data range must be fewer than 180 days # Example without rfid_file (by default NA)  report_gfdata(user,   pass,   exp = "StudyName",   unit = 1,   start_date = "2023-01-01",   end_date = Sys.Date(),   input_type = "daily",   save_dir = tempdir(),   plot_opt = "All" ) } # Create a Final Report in PDF format from the finalized data received from C-Lock Inc. # Note that Unit could be numeric or character (It will use to print in the PDF)  # File is a list of reports from C-Lock inc. # it could be one or multiples depend on the number of units file <- list(system.file("extdata", "StudyName_FinalReport.xlsx", package = "greenfeedr"))  # By default `report_gfdata()` plot only methane (CH4), but here we defined to plot "All" gases  # Is it possible to include a file with Farm IDs to use in the reports # The file structure should be: FarmName | RFID  report_gfdata(   user = NA,   pass = NA,   exp = "StudyName",   unit = 1,   start_date = "2024-05-13",   end_date = "2024-05-25",   input_type = "final",   save_dir = tempdir(),   plot_opt = "All",   rfid_file = NA,   file_path = file ) #> No valid data provided. Please include a valid 'rfid_file' parameter. #>  #>  #> processing file: FinalReportsGF.Rmd #> 1/8                               #> 2/8 [libraries]                   #> 3/8                               #> 4/8 [Data per cow]                #> 5/8                               #> 6/8 [Data per day]                #> 7/8                               #> 8/8 [Gas data production per cow] #> output file: FinalReportsGF.knit.md #> /opt/hostedtoolcache/pandoc/3.1.11/x64/pandoc +RTS -K512m -RTS FinalReportsGF.knit.md --to latex --from markdown+autolink_bare_uris+tex_math_single_backslash --output /tmp/RtmpOT62S6/FinalReport_StudyName.tex --lua-filter /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/rmarkdown/rmarkdown/lua/pagebreak.lua --lua-filter /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/rmarkdown/rmarkdown/lua/latex-div.lua --embed-resources --standalone --highlight-style tango --pdf-engine pdflatex --variable graphics --variable 'geometry:margin=1in'  #> Warning: error in running command #> ! sh: 1: pdflatex: not found #> Error: LaTeX failed to compile /tmp/RtmpOT62S6/FinalReport_StudyName.tex. See https://yihui.org/tinytex/r/#debugging for debugging tips. See FinalReport_StudyName.log for more info.

title: Process GreenFeed Visits — viseat
text: Processes GreenFeed visits food drops.      Generates list animals visiting GreenFeed      description animals visiting system requested period.

title: Process GreenFeed Visits — viseat
text: file_path File feedtimes C-Lock. multiple files provided, units order unit GreenFeed unit number(s). multiple units, vector, list, character "1,2" start_date Start date study end_date End date study rfid_file file contains RFID animals enrolled study. order col1=FarmName col2=RFID

title: Process GreenFeed Visits — viseat
text: list two data frames: visits_per_unit Data frame daily processed GreenFeed data, including columns FarmName, Date, Time, number drops, visits. visits_per_animal Data frame weekly processed GreenFeed data, including columns FarmName, total drops, total visits, mean drops, mean visits.

title: Process GreenFeed Visits — viseat
code: # You should provide the 'feedtimes' files. # it could be a list of files if you have data from multiple units to combine file <- list(system.file("extdata", "feedtimes.csv", package = "greenfeedr"))  # If the user include an RFID file, the structure should be in col1 the farmname or visualID, and # col2 the RFID or TAG_ID. The file could be save in different formats (.xlsx, .csv, or .txt). RFIDs <- system.file("extdata", "RFID_file.csv", package = "greenfeedr")  data <- viseat(file,   unit = 1,   start_date = "2024-05-13",   end_date = "2024-05-25",   rfid_file = RFIDs ) #> Rows: 6479 Columns: 9 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (2): FeedTime, CowTag #> dbl (7): CurrentCup, MaxCups, CurrentPeriod, MaxPeriods, CupDelay, PeriodDel... #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> Animal IDs not visiting GF:

title: greenfeedr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
